intent: To provide a centralized platform for creating, managing, and delivering educational content to students while tracking progress and performance metrics.

entities.name: User Management Module
entities.description: Handles authentication and authorization for administrators, instructors, and students via role-based access control.

entities.name: Content Repository
entities.description: A centralized database and storage system for managing diverse media formats including video, text, and interactive quizzes.

entities.name: Learning Management Engine
entities.description: Orchestrates course delivery, progress tracking, and performance analytics for students.

entities.name: Integration Gateway
entities.description: Provides secure APIs to connect with external grading, certification, and third-party software systems.

entities.name: Reporting Service
entities.description: Aggregates performance metrics and generates progress reports for instructors and administrators.

architecture.description: The platform functions by authenticating users through the User Management Module to determine their role. Instructors upload and organize content within the Content Repository, which is then served via a mobile-responsive interface by the Learning Management Engine. As students consume content and complete assessments, the engine tracks their progress in real-time. Performance data is sent to the Reporting Service for visualization and synced with external entities through the Integration Gateway for certification and grade finalization.

endpoints.summary: Authenticate user and return session token
Retrieve student progress for a specific course

validation_rules: metrics must be a valid JSON

endpoints.summary: Generate performance analytics report for a specific user
Trigger synchronization with external certification systems

rules.description: Restrict content modification based on role
rules.logic: User must possess the Instructor role to execute create, update, or delete operations on the Content Repository.
A student's progress percentage must be stored as an immutable cumulative value that only increments based on the completion of unique content nodes.
Access to administrative reports is restricted to users with the Administrator role; Instructors are restricted to viewing data associated only with their assigned courses.
Final grades must be validated against the completed assessment count before being dispatched through the Integration Gateway to third-party systems.
Content must pass a validation check for media file integrity and associated quiz mappings before transitioning from 'Draft' to 'Published' status.
